script_or_copy: Headline (top, white on dark gradient): 'हॉटस्पॉट के झंझट मिटाने हैं?' (Hotspot ke jhanjhat mitane hain?). Sub-line (white on magenta band): 'घर का नेट रिचार्ज से चलाना है' (Ghar ka net recharge se chalana hai). MID-FRAME GRAPHIC OVERLAY: the word 'Hotspot' rendered with a RED STRIKETHROUGH X across it (visual negation device, added in production — not in original brief). Locked Wiom lockup (bottom, magenta footer): brand mark 'Wiom' + 'रिचार्ज वाला घर का नेट' (Recharge wala ghar ka net) + sub 'जितनी ज़रूरत, उतना रिचार्ज' (Jitni zaroorat, utna recharge — JZUR, NOT JDRUDN) + pink house-with-WiFi icon.
visual_description: Real-feeling middle-class North Indian living room interior. Strong household composition with THREE family members visible: (1) father (foreground left, ~40s, white kurta, scratching head with one hand, holding phone with other, frustrated expression); (2) mother (mid-ground, saree, holding a glass, tired/resigned expression near a side cabinet); (3) daughter (foreground right, ~12, striped t-shirt, at a laptop showing a loading/buffering WiFi icon). Background: TV showing a loading/buffering circle, side cabinet with religious shelf, lamp, sofa with cushions, books and notebooks open on the central low table, a second phone resting on the table. Lived-in, real, NOT aspirational. The combined visual reads: 'whole-family struggle with one device/hotspot.' NB: original S3 brief specified 'ONE phone at the centre... one person strained, daily bottleneck.' Produced version uses a multi-person family composition, which is closer to the S9 (family simultaneity) brief's 'whole family leaning on ONE hotspot at once.'
notes: Produced version of S3 / JUN26-T-015 (Hotspot exhaustion static, L3 Workaround-pain, peer = household head). User flagged: 'might have drifted a bit because we tried to improve messaging in iteration.' Two iteration choices to evaluate: (1) multi-person household composition (drifted toward S9 family-simultaneity territory vs original 'one person strained' brief — affects need-state sweep attribution); (2) the 'Hotspot' RED STRIKETHROUGH graphic overlay (added; reinforces 'mitane hain' negation). Also evaluate L3 doctrine fit, ghar/category landing, and the wave-wide JDRUDN-absent pattern.
